name: [Math] Treating '=' as 'do the operation' only
description: Equals sign as process, not balance
remediationBody: The equals sign means both sides have the same value. Whatever you do to one side, do to the other to keep balance.

name: [Math] Multiplying always makes numbers bigger
remediationBody: Multiplying by a fraction or decimal between 0 and 1 makes a positive number smaller.

name: [Math] Larger denominator means larger fraction
remediationBody: For the same numerator, a larger denominator means smaller pieces, so the fraction is smaller.

name: [Math] Adding numerators and denominators
remediationBody: You need a common denominator (or clear meaning) before adding parts of a fraction.

name: [Math] Subtracting a negative makes the result smaller
remediationBody: Subtracting a negative is the same as adding its opposite; the result moves right on the number line.

name: [Math] Area vs perimeter confusion
remediationBody: Perimeter is distance around; area is space inside. Use different units and formulas.

name: [Math] Percent increase then decrease returns to start
description: Percent change not reversible by same percent
remediationBody: Percents apply to different bases; going up 20% then down 20% does not return to the original.

name: [Math] Square is always larger than rectangle (same perimeter)
remediationBody: For a fixed perimeter, a square maximizes area among rectangles—but compare actual dimensions.

name: [Math] 0.25 is smaller than 0.3 because 25 > 3
remediationBody: Compare place by place: 0.30 vs 0.25; tenths dominate hundredths here.

name: [Math] Division always makes numbers smaller
remediationBody: Dividing by a number between 0 and 1 (for positives) increases the value.

name: [Grammar] Subject-verb agreement with 'each'
remediationBody: Treat 'each' as singular: 'Each student has' not 'Each student have'.

name: [Grammar] Its vs it's
remediationBody: It's = it is. Its = possessive (no apostrophe).

name: [Grammar] Affect vs effect
remediationBody: Usually: affect (verb) = influence; effect (noun) = result. Exceptions exist—check a dictionary.

name: [Grammar] Who vs whom
remediationBody: Who acts as subject; whom as object. Try substituting he/him to test.

name: [Grammar] Fewer vs less
remediationBody: Fewer for countable items; less for mass nouns ('less water', 'fewer cups').

name: [Grammar] Then vs than
remediationBody: Then = time/order. Than = comparison.

name: [Grammar] Lie vs lay
remediationBody: Lay needs an object (lay the book). Lie does not (I lie down).

name: [Grammar] Good vs well
remediationBody: Good describes nouns; well often describes how an action is done (exceptions: 'feel well').

name: [Science] Heavier objects always fall faster
remediationBody: In many classroom cases, air resistance matters; in vacuum, acceleration due to gravity is the same for mass.

name: [Science] Heat and temperature are the same
remediationBody: Temperature relates to average particle motion; heat is energy transferred due to temperature difference.

name: [Science] Plants get most mass from soil
remediationBody: Most plant dry mass comes from carbon dioxide converted into sugars, not from soil alone.

name: [Science] Seasons caused by Earth-Sun distance
remediationBody: Seasons are mostly from Earth's axial tilt changing sunlight angle and day length.

name: [Science] Evolution 'wants' a species to improve
remediationBody: Evolution describes change in populations over generations via variation and selection—not goals.

name: [Science] Electricity only flows if electrons 'run out'
remediationBody: In a steady circuit, current is a loop; charge is pushed around; components transform energy.

name: [Science] Ozone hole is the same as climate change
remediationBody: Ozone depletion and greenhouse warming are distinct problems with some linked chemistry but different causes/effects.

name: [Science] Batteries store electrons like a tank
remediationBody: Batteries store chemical energy that drives charge through a circuit; it's not a bucket of electrons.

name: [General] Reading a graph: confusing slope with height
remediationBody: Check axes labels: slope is rate of change; a point's height is a value at an input.

name: [General] Correlation implies causation
remediationBody: Correlation can be driven by a third variable. Look for study design and mechanisms.

name: [General] Average always represents a typical student
remediationBody: Means hide spread and outliers; median or context may be more representative.

name: [General] Speed vs velocity are interchangeable
remediationBody: Velocity includes direction; speed is magnitude only.

name: [General] If pH is 7, a solution has no ions
remediationBody: Neutral water still has small concentrations of H+ and OH- ions.

name: [General] DNA is only in the nucleus in eukaryotes
remediationBody: Mitochondria/chloroplasts also contain DNA; prokaryotes have nucleoid regions.

name: [General] Natural means safe and synthetic means harmful
remediationBody: Toxicity depends on dose and substance, not the label natural vs synthetic.

name: [General] Energy is 'used up' in a machine
remediationBody: Energy transforms; efficiency limits useful output, but energy is conserved in accounting.
